title: The Art of Knowing When to Quit
subtitle: 适时放弃的智慧

paragraphs.en: Projects, relationships, and jobs don't always work out as planned — and it's not always clear when to give up. We've all repeatedly heard the saying that 'quitters never win', but sometimes the best decision is to cut our losses. Think about that relationship you held on to even after it had run its course or the job you stayed in despite your boss making you miserable.
paragraphs.zh: 项目、人际关系和工作并不总按计划进行——何时放弃也并非总是显而易见。我们都反复听过“放弃者永不成功”这句话，但有时最佳决策是及时止损。想想那段你明知已到尽头却仍紧抓不放的关系，或那份老板让你痛苦不堪却仍坚守的工作。

paragraphs.en: Many of us try to hold onto the last ray of hope, thinking we can make it work, whatever 'it' is. And while perseverance is generally an excellent quality, it may also incline you to over-invest in something that is no longer a good idea. As an executive coach, I work with numerous passionate and hard-working leaders who don't want to quit.
paragraphs.zh: 我们许多人试图抓住最后一丝希望，以为无论“它”是什么都能挽回。虽然毅力通常是优秀品质，但它也可能让你在已不值得的事情上过度投入。作为高管教练，我与许多不愿“放弃”的充满激情且勤奋的领导者共事。

paragraphs.en: But the smartest leaders learn to discern the difference between quitting too soon and holding onto something that they shouldn't. We are all susceptible to the sunk-cost delusion, an unconscious bias that leads us to persist in an endeavor we have already invested time, effort, or money into — even when abandoning it would be more beneficial.
paragraphs.zh: 但最聪明的领导者学会辨别过早放弃与执着于不该坚持之事之间的区别。我们都容易受到沉没成本错觉的影响，这是一种无意识偏见，让我们即使放弃更有利，仍执着于已投入时间、精力或金钱的事情。

paragraphs.en: We realize that we can't get back the resources we've given, so we persevere — investing in a project that should be halted, staying in a relationship that should be over, or finishing a book we no longer enjoy and should put down, because we're already halfway through it. If quitting is not your thing, you may be especially susceptible to this bias.
paragraphs.zh: 我们意识到已投入的资源无法收回，于是继续坚持——投资本应停止的项目，维持本应结束的关系，或读完一本已不喜欢且该放下的书，只因已读了一半。如果放弃不是你的风格，你可能特别容易受这种偏见影响。

words.meaning: adj. 易受影响的，敏感的

paragraphs.en: The sunk-cost delusion causes us to overly worry about what we'll lose if we move on and not think enough about the costs of not moving on. To offset this bias, deliberately refocus your thinking on the gains so you can more objectively weigh the alternatives. Ask yourself: What might I gain by cutting my losses now?
paragraphs.zh: 沉没成本错觉让我们过度担忧前进会失去什么，而忽略了停滞不前的代价。为抵消这种偏见，有意识地将思维重新聚焦于收益，以便更客观地权衡选项。问问自己：现在止损我能获得什么？

paragraphs.en: Will you be happier or have time for another opportunity? Then ask yourself: What will it cost me to soldier on? For example, is it possible that you'll be throwing more good money after what you've already lost? Or that you won't have the energy to capitalize on other promising possibilities?
paragraphs.zh: 你会更快乐还是有时间迎接另一个机会？然后问自己：继续坚持要付出什么代价？例如，你是否可能在已损失的基础上继续投入更多？或者你将没有精力去把握其他有前途的可能？

paragraphs.en: The illusion of control can also interfere with our best judgment, leading us to overestimate our ability to control events and attain a positive outcome. This bias gives us a sense of agency and can promote mental health. However, the bias can also result in escalating commitment to a losing course of action.
paragraphs.zh: 控制幻觉也会干扰我们的最佳判断，让我们高估自己控制事件和获得积极结果的能力。这种偏见赋予我们掌控感，有助于心理健康。然而，它也可能导致对失败行动路线的不断加码。

paragraphs.en: Especially when combined with an 'I'm not a quitter' mentality, feeling like we have more control than we do puts us at risk of doubling down when we should pull out. To counteract this bias, consider your situation and make a simple two-column list of what's in your control and what's not. Think rigorously.
paragraphs.zh: 尤其是与“我不是轻言放弃者”的心态结合时，感觉自己拥有超出实际的掌控力，会让我们在本该抽身时反而加倍下注。为抵消这种偏见，审视你的处境，做一个简单的两栏清单，列出你能控制的和不能控制的。严格思考。

paragraphs.en: While you may be able to influence other people and various circumstances, you can't force them to change or go your way. Getting clear on what you can control and what you can't is essential to making a quality decision about whether to call it quits or persevere. With a written list in hand, you can ensure you focus on what you can control and better assess whether your continued efforts are worth the expense.
paragraphs.zh: 虽然你可能影响他人和各种环境，但无法强迫他们改变或顺从你的意愿。厘清可控与不可控之事，对于做出放弃还是坚持的优质决策至关重要。有了书面清单，你就能确保专注于可控之事，并更好地评估持续努力是否值得。

paragraphs.en: Expending effort and emotion on things you can't control can be both draining and disempowering. Research indicates that people link their self-identity and social status to their commitments. And because we identify with our commitments, withdrawing from one can feel like a threat to our identity or status. Take my client Ryan, a leader at a technology company.
paragraphs.zh: 将精力和情感耗费在不可控之事上，既令人筋疲力尽又让人丧失力量。研究表明，人们将自我认同和社会地位与自己的承诺挂钩。由于我们认同自己的承诺，退出其中一项可能会感觉是对身份或地位的威胁。以我的客户瑞安为例，她是一家科技公司的领导者。

paragraphs.en: Ryan had invested two years and a lot of effort in a design project that wasn't delivering the needed outcomes, but the thought of abandoning it after all she had put in was hard to bear. As Ryan and I explored whether she should cancel the initiative, she stated, 'The thing is, I'm not a quitter.' This singular, fixed sense of self was clouding her judgment and limiting her choices for action.
paragraphs.zh: 瑞安在一个未能产出预期成果的设计项目上投入了两年时间和大量精力，但想到要放弃她已付出的一切，她难以承受。当我和瑞安探讨是否应取消该项目时，她说：“问题是，我不是一个轻言放弃的人。”这种单一、固化的自我认知模糊了她的判断，限制了她的行动选择。

paragraphs.en: Our self-concept and identity are mental concepts that influence our behavior. If you strongly identify as not being a quitter, you can end up staying stuck in a course of action due to your narrow and rigid self-concept. To overcome this kind of internal obstacle, recall your other positive traits and personality characteristics.
paragraphs.zh: 我们的自我概念和身份是影响行为的心理构念。如果你强烈认同自己不是放弃者，就可能因狭隘僵化的自我概念而陷入困境。为克服这种内在障碍，回忆你的其他积极特质和个性特征。

paragraphs.en: Ask yourself: What additional strengths do I have that would be helpful to employ here? For example, when I asked Ryan what other positive qualities she needed to leverage for decision making, she replied, 'Discernment and bravery.' Now, examine your situation through the lens of each of these facets of yourself. What does the discerning part of you say? The brave part?
paragraphs.zh: 问问自己：我还有哪些额外优势可以在此处发挥作用？例如，当我问瑞安需要运用哪些其他积极品质来决策时，她回答：“洞察力和勇敢。”现在，从自己的各个侧面审视你的处境。你洞察的那一面怎么说？勇敢的那一面呢？

paragraphs.en: Considering your decision through these different characteristics will enable you to see that you have more choices. It's also helpful to seek external sources of information, rather than relying solely on your thinking and instincts. But it's important to think through whose opinions you solicit. Ideally, seek out people who are less invested in your decision than you are.
paragraphs.zh: 通过这些不同特质考量你的决定，会让你看到自己拥有更多选择。寻求外部信息来源也有帮助，而非仅依赖自己的思维和直觉。但重要的是想清楚你征求谁的意见。理想情况下，寻找那些对你的决定投入比你少的人。

paragraphs.en: If you're considering whether to shut down your entrepreneurial venture, for instance, reach out to other founders as opposed to just your business partner. Similarly, if you're looking for love advice, ask someone removed from the ups and downs of your relationship instead of relying only on your best friend. In all cases, I recommend describing your situation and following up with a question like: 'How would you manage this situation?'
paragraphs.zh: 例如，若你正考虑是否关闭创业项目，去联系其他创始人而非仅咨询你的商业伙伴。同样，若寻求恋爱建议，去问一个远离你们关系起伏的人，而不是只依赖最好的朋友。在所有情况下，我建议描述你的处境并追问：“你会如何处理这种情况？”

paragraphs.en: Alternately, you could also seek out hard data that illuminates your real chance of success in whatever endeavor you are contemplating. Sticking with the example of the entrepreneurial venture, numbers are your best friend. Use them to remove yourself from the love you have for this idea and consider your burn rate relative to growth plan and industry growth rates.
paragraphs.zh: 或者，你也可以寻找能揭示你所考虑之事真实成功概率的确凿数据。继续以创业为例，数字是你最好的朋友。用数字让自己从对想法的热爱中抽离，考虑烧钱速度相对于增长计划和行业增长率的情况。

paragraphs.en: External sources of information can help you see angles and possibilities you might not have considered. When something that seemed so promising at the start doesn't turn out the way we imagined, we can become overly self-critical. Nobody likes to feel that they've made a poor decision, especially if making intelligent decisions is a quality you hold in high esteem.
paragraphs.zh: 外部信息源能帮你看到可能未曾考虑的角度和可能性。当起初看似大有希望的事未能如我们所愿发展时，我们可能变得过度自我批评。没人喜欢觉得自己做了糟糕的决定，尤其是当你高度重视明智决策这一品质时。

paragraphs.en: The most effective strategy here is to give yourself a break. Practicing self-compassion increases your resilience, emotional intelligence, compassion toward others, and can boost your performance as well as help you develop a growth mindset. Instead of deliberating and criticizing yourself, focus instead on what you can learn from this situation.
paragraphs.zh: 最有效的策略是让自己喘口气。践行自我同情能增强你的韧性、情商和对他人的同理心，还能提升你的表现并帮助你培养成长型思维。与其反复纠结和自我批评，不如专注于你能从中学到什么。

words.meaning: n. 自我同情，自我慈悲

paragraphs.en: For example, Ryan recognized she had developed various new skills and leadership capabilities from leading the design project. And despite ultimately deciding to drop it, she was still happy she had tried and grateful for what the experience taught her. Undeniably, perseverance can help us succeed.
paragraphs.zh: 例如，瑞安认识到自己通过领导设计项目培养了各种新技能和领导能力。尽管最终决定放弃，她仍为尝试过而高兴，并对这段经历教会她的一切心怀感激。不可否认，坚持不懈能帮助我们成功。

paragraphs.en: But for your well-being, it's important to learn how to distinguish quitting too soon and clinging to a losing course of action. While letting go can be difficult, it will free up your time, energy, and mental space to imagine new possibilities and pursue new opportunities.
paragraphs.zh: 但为了你的身心健康，学会区分过早放弃与执着于失败行动至关重要。放手虽难，却能释放你的时间、精力和心理空间，去想象新的可能并追求新的机遇。
